exclusivity_model: Full exclusive (venue OR promoter must use TM)
primary_markets: USA, UK, Ireland, Mexico, Canada, Australia, Germany (partial)
sports_partners: Arsenal, Tottenham, West Ham, Brentford, Crystal Palace, Everton, Wolves, Chelsea, Bolton, Wigan (EPL/EFL); West Ham (London Stadium); Premiership Rugby (5yr deal); Rugby World Cup 2027/2029; World Athletics 2025 Tokyo; Qatar Stars League (QSL); Olympiacos FC; Las Vegas Raiders (Allegiant Stadium); Inter Miami CF; MLS Official (29 clubs); Dallas Cowboys; Golden State Warriors
music_venues: House of Blues chain, Fillmore venues, Palladium chains, 275+ owned/operated US venues
key_leverage: Promoter arm withholds tour routing from non-TM venues. DOJ consent decree (Mar 2026) caps new exclusives at 4 yrs, divests 13 amphitheater exclusives
how_to_detect: Venue buy-ticket button goes to ticketmaster.com or am.ticketmaster.com subdomain
openness_to_tixr: Very Low (locked). Post-DOJ settlement venues expiring are opportunity

exclusivity_model: Exclusive via AEG venue ownership; voluntary exclusive deals elsewhere
primary_markets: USA, UK, Germany, Sweden, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, Thailand
sports_partners: LA28 Olympics (w/ Eventim); B.League Japan; Swedish Elite Football; Venues New South Wales; BNP Paribas Open tennis; WM Phoenix Open golf; Coachella; Red Rocks; O2 Arena London; AO Arena Manchester; Uber Arena Berlin; Barclays Arena Hamburg; Accor Arena Paris; UOB Live Bangkok; Mercedes-Benz Arena Shanghai; IG Arena Nagoya
music_venues: The O2 London, OVO Wembley, Bataclan Paris, adidas Arena Paris, Dreamland Margate, BST Hyde Park, All Points East
key_leverage: AEG owns venues outright — switching requires AEG partnership termination. Expanding aggressively in Germany/SE Asia
how_to_detect: Buy-ticket button goes to axs.com or axs.com/[eu/uk/etc] or venue-branded AXS white label
openness_to_tixr: Very Low (AEG-owned). Non-AEG venues using AXS are weaker exclusives — opportunity exists

exclusivity_model: Dominant in Germany/Europe via ticketing system + venue ownership + promoter arm
primary_markets: Germany (dominant ~70%), Austria, Switzerland, Netherlands, Italy, Poland, Scandinavia, Brazil, Chile
sports_partners: EHF (European Handball) 2022–2028 all Euros; 100+ European football clubs (top leagues); handball/basketball/ice hockey European clubs; LA28 Olympics (co-provider with AXS)
music_venues: Hamburg Barclays Arena co-operator; Olympiahalle Munich; various DACH venues; acquired See Tickets (Jun 2024) adding 15 offices across Europe + US
key_leverage: Controls ticketing infrastructure in Germany — venues using EVENTIM system are sticky. See Tickets acquisition expanded UK/FR/NL/ES footprint
how_to_detect: eventim.de or eventim.co.uk as buy link; local white-label eventim-branded URL
openness_to_tixr: Low in DACH core. Medium in newer markets (LATAM, Eastern Europe, SEA) where grip is weaker

exclusivity_model: Official partner (primary ticketing) for select teams; otherwise secondary marketplace
primary_markets: USA primary; UK growing (~50% EPL clubs); international expanding
sports_partners: Dallas Cowboys; NFL official; ~50% EPL clubs (Liverpool FC, Watford, Leeds, Wolverhampton, Brentford — note: some overlap with TM); MLB official marketplace; Big 12 Conference (official); NASCAR; Florida Panthers (NHL); Cleveland Cavaliers (NBA); NY Broadway + London West End
music_venues: Growing venue integrations via Paciolan (160 college athletics + 100+ pro venues + 75 arts)
key_leverage: Offered 'retaliation insurance' to win TM venues post-DOJ. API-first, developer-friendly. Less coercive than TM/AXS
how_to_detect: seatgeek.com as buy link; team/venue portal with SeatGeek branding
openness_to_tixr: Medium — direct competitor in US, but less entrenched internationally. Some venue types (festivals, boutique) still open

exclusivity_model: Non-exclusive in most cases; official partner agreements for key festivals/venues
primary_markets: UK (primary), France, Netherlands, Belgium, Spain, Germany, USA (NY/LA/Nashville)
sports_partners: Professional Darts Corporation (PDC); Houses of Parliament events
music_venues: Glastonbury Festival; Universal Music Group events; SJM Concerts; Tomorrowland; Rock in Rio Lisboa; Boardmasters; Alexandra Palace; Electric Ballroom; Leadmill Sheffield; Albert Hall Manchester; 50+ UK independent venues
key_leverage: Dominant in UK festival/grassroots market. Now folded into Eventim ecosystem — expanding synergies
how_to_detect: seetickets.com buy link or seetickets.us (US); often used for independent UK venues
openness_to_tixr: Medium-High — mid-tier venues using See Tickets are often less locked in than TM/AXS

exclusivity_model: Exclusive partnerships with boutique/independent venues and electronic music scene
primary_markets: UK, USA (NYC/LA), Germany, Italy, France, Spain, India (Mumbai/Bangalore)
sports_partners: Minimal — focused on music/nightlife. The Ave (announced Feb 2026 as official ticketing partner)
music_venues: Berghain Berlin; Fabric London; Motion Bristol; EartH London; Printworks London; Village Underground; 10K+ venues globally
key_leverage: Strong in electronic music / club scene. Anti-touting tech (tickets tied to fan identity) appeals to independent venues
how_to_detect: dice.fm as primary buy link; venue uses DICE Fan ID system
openness_to_tixr: High for DICE-served venues — these are exactly Tixr's target (premium independent, non-corporate)

primary_markets: Global (180+ countries) — dominant in SME/independent event market
sports_partners: Grassroots sports, community events, amateur leagues
music_venues: Smaller independent venues, pop-ups, community venues globally
key_leverage: Very low — any venue can switch. Tixr's premium positioning is a direct upgrade for Eventbrite venues that outgrow the platform
openness_to_tixr: Very High — no exclusivity, natural upgrade path for venues that want Tixr's features

exclusivity_model: Open distribution (no exclusives); signed 'open distribution' deals with 100+ venues/teams
sports_partners: Manchester City, Sevilla, NY Yankees, Alpine F1, 100+ venues; MLB league deal (open distribution)
music_venues: Hamilton Broadway, Book of Mormon (ATG Entertainment); 100+ venues
key_leverage: Shifting industry toward open distribution model — challenges Ticketmaster's exclusive primary monopoly
how_to_detect: viagogo.com or stubhub.com as resale platform; 'also available on StubHub' language on venue site
openness_to_tixr: N/A — secondary market; but open distribution trend it's driving creates Tixr entry points

exclusivity_model: Exclusive international sports deals — separate from US consumer TM
primary_markets: UK, Europe, Middle East (Qatar), Asia (Japan - via PIA partnership)
sports_partners: Qatar Stars League (all 12 clubs digital); Olympiacos FC Greece; Rugby World Cup 2027/2029; Women's Rugby World Cup 2025 England; Premiership Rugby (5yr); Bolton Wanderers; Wigan Athletic+Warriors; Crystal Palace; West Ham; ESSMA partnership; MLB London; Cape Town HSBC Rugby Sevens; Danish Cycling Federation
music_venues: Large UK arenas not owned by AEG
key_leverage: International white-label deals — partners use TM's system under their own brand
openness_to_tixr: Low for existing TM Sport clients. Gulf/MENA region has weaker TM grip — opportunity

exclusivity_model: Regional dominant in Chile; non-exclusive elsewhere in LATAM
sports_partners: Chilean football clubs; Copa Chile events
key_leverage: Local relationships in Chile; but no international backing. Tixr's premium tech would be superior
openness_to_tixr: High — regional player without TM/AXS resources. Mid-tier LATAM venues could switch

exclusivity_model: Non-exclusive retail-based; shares tickets with other platforms
sports_partners: French Ligue 1 clubs (partial); French Open tennis (Roland Garros supplement)
music_venues: French concert halls, theatres, festivals — supplement to main ticketer
key_leverage: Retail distribution network (800+ FNAC stores); but digital-first platforms are eroding this
how_to_detect: fnacspectacles.com as one of multiple purchase options
openness_to_tixr: High — France has non-exclusive norms for most venues

exclusivity_model: Exclusive deals in Australia/NZ; partial in LATAM
sports_partners: Rugby Australia; Cricket Australia; AFL; NRL; A-League football
music_venues: Sydney Entertainment Centre; major AU/NZ arenas
key_leverage: Dominant in AU/NZ sports ticketing; less entrenched than TM globally
how_to_detect: ticketek.com.au or ticketek.co.nz as buy link
openness_to_tixr: Low in AU/NZ core. Medium in their LATAM expansion where local players dominate

exclusivity_model: Exclusive primary ticketer for US college athletics
sports_partners: 160+ college athletic programs (80% of Power Five); 100+ pro sports/arenas; 75 performing arts; 120M+ tickets/year
music_venues: University arenas and performing arts centers
key_leverage: Near-monopoly on US college sports ticketing. SeatGeek partnership for secondary. Not international.
how_to_detect: paciolan.com powered portal; university athletics websites
openness_to_tixr: N/A — USA college focus, not Tixr's international expansion target
